hero.subtitle: Discover the Gulf
hero.title: Kuwait: Where Tradition Meets Tomorrow
hero.description: Experience the perfect blend of modern luxury and deep cultural heritage in the heart of the Arabian Peninsula.

categories.title: Explore by Category
categories.description: Find your perfect experience in Kuwait

page.subtitle: EXPLORE THE HERITAGE
page.title: Discover the Soul of Kuwait
page.description: From the bustling souqs of the past to the modern marvels of today, explore the hidden gems of the Gulf.

experiences.title: Featured Experiences
experiences.description: Immerse yourself in the unique activities that make Kuwait unforgettable, from the desert to the sea.

grand_mosque.title: Grand Mosque Guided Tour
grand_mosque.description: Guided tour through the largest mosque in Kuwait, showcasing Islamic architecture and grandeur.

failaka.title: Failaka Island Heritage
failaka.description: Explore the historical ruins and ancient Greek temples on this significant Kuwaiti island.

mirror_house.title: Mirror House Art Gallery
mirror_house.description: Visit the world-renowned private museum entirely covered in intricate mirrored mosaics.

salmi_desert.title: Al-Salmi Desert Stars
salmi_desert.description: Experience a magical night of stargazing under the clear skies of the Kuwaiti desert.

kuwait_towers.title: Kuwait Towers
kuwait_towers.location: Arabian Gulf Street, Sharq District, Kuwait City
kuwait_towers.description: An iconic symbol of modern Kuwait, offering panoramic views of the city and the Arabian Gulf.

details.about: The Kuwait Towers are a group of three slender towers in Kuwait City, standing on a promontory into the Persian Gulf. They were the sixth, and last, group in the larger system of the Kuwait Water Towers of 33 towers, and were built in a style differing from the other five groups. The Kuwait Towers were officially inaugurated in March 1979 and are regarded as a landmark and symbol of modern Kuwait.
details.about_extended: The main tower is 187 metres (614 ft) high and carries two spheres. The lower sphere holds in its bottom half a water tank of 4,500 cubic metres and in its upper half there is a restaurant that accommodates 90 people, a café, a lounge and a reception hall. The upper sphere, which rises to 123 metres (404 ft) above sea level and completes a full turn every 30 minutes, holds a café.

mubarakiya.title: Souq Al-Mubarakiya
mubarakiya.description: Experience the traditional atmosphere, shop for spices, and enjoy authentic Kuwaiti cuisine.

avenues.title: The Avenues
avenues.description: The largest shopping mall in Kuwait, featuring international brands, theme parks, and cinema.

grand_mosque_alt.title: Grand Mosque
grand_mosque_alt.description: A stunning example of Islamic architecture, featuring intricate calligraphy and massive chandeliers.

shaheed_park.title: Al Shaheed Park
shaheed_park.description: The largest urban park in Kuwait, offering beautiful landscapes, museums, and botanical gardens.

scientific_center.title: The Scientific Center
scientific_center.description: Home to one of the largest aquariums in the Middle East, an IMAX theater, and interactive exhibits.

al_boom.title: Al Boom Steak and Seafood Restaurant
al_boom.description: Savor the charcoal-grilled seafood, steak and other specialties of Al Boom. Diners will want to experience this unique dining venue aboard a traditional Arabic dhow. Beautiful teak with gold-leaf accents gives this landlocked vessel the atmosphere of luxury sailing.

map.title: Navigate the City's Landmarks
map.description: Plan your itinerary with our interactive map. Discover hidden gems, find the best routes, and explore districts like a local.

footer.description: The official guide to exploring the hidden gems of the Gulf. Experience tradition, luxury, and adventure.
